pi_items.metadata_value: Images downloaded from Unsplash with fallback to Python-generated synthetic images
pi_items.verified_value: Primary: Real Unsplash images (accessible, verified). Fallback: Python PIL generates synthetic test image if downloads fail (setup_background_task.sh lines 24-37)

privileged_info_summary: The task remove_background@1 uses real Unsplash images as primary data source (both URLs verified accessible) with fallback to synthetic generation. The verification thresholds are precisely specified: background transparency ≥70%, subject opacity ≥85%, pass threshold 75% (requiring 3/4 criteria met). Alpha channel values define transparency as <50 and opacity as >200. CRITICAL CONTRADICTION: The setup script claims to download 'image with clear subject on white background', but the actual primary Unsplash image contains only 0.01% white pixels and has a tan/yellow color scheme - it is not a white background image suitable for the stated task. The task.json lacks a metadata field present in other similar tasks.
